description_long: This is a compact, freestanding clock tower with a cylindrical upper section mounted on a broad, square pedestal base. The clock face is circular, bordered with a gold ring, and features Roman numerals and two ornate black hands, set against a weathered, off-white background. The main body and base are constructed from a dark, marble-like stone with visible veining, giving the structure a sense of weight and permanence. The rear and sides of the tower are solid and unadorned, emphasizing the prominence of the clock face on the front. The overall design is simple yet stately, reminiscent of neoclassical or early 20th-century civic architecture. There are no visible openings, doors, or decorative elements beyond the clock face itself.
description: A small, cylindrical clock tower with a marble-like black stone body and a single ornate clock face with Roman numerals set in a gold ring. The structure sits on a broad, square pedestal base and has a stately, neoclassical appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the ornate clock face with Roman numerals, gold trim, and black hands, set in a cylindrical black stone body on a square base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the solid, unadorned cylindrical body and the marble-like texture of the stone, with no visible clock face or openings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, rounded back of the cylinder and the broad, square base, with no clock face or decorative features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the solid cylindrical body and the marble texture, again with no visible clock face.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular with a gold outer ring, featuring black Roman numerals and two ornate black hands. The background is off-white and weathered, with no minute markers or additional decorative elements.
